elements.2412-15289_sent_df990370: Large language models (LLMs) have made significant advancements across various tasks, but their safety alignment remain a major concern.
elements.2412-15289_sent_1b6b5ffb: Exploring jailbreak prompts can expose LLMs' vulnerabilities and guide efforts to secure them.
elements.2412-15289_sent_d84eec96: Existing methods primarily design sophisticated instructions for the LLM to follow, or rely on multiple iterations, which could hinder the performance and efficiency of jailbreaks.
elements.2412-15289_sent_35506d3f: In this work, we propose a novel jailbreak paradigm, \ul{S}imple \ul{A}ssistive \ul{T}ask Link\ul{a}ge (SATA), which can effectively circumvent LLM safeguards and elicit harmful responses.
elements.2412-15289_sent_4fad4915: Specifically, SATA first masks harmful keywords within a malicious query to generate a relatively benign query containing one or multiple [MASK] special tokens.
elements.2412-15289_sent_34ee0e3b: It then employs a simple assistive task—such as a masked language model task or an element lookup by position task—to encode the semantics of the masked keywords.
elements.2412-15289_sent_4678bb09: Finally, SATA links the assistive task with the masked query to jointly perform the jailbreak.
elements.2412-15289_sent_4aa524e2: Extensive experiments show that SATA achieves state-of-the-art performance and outperforms baselines by a large margin.
elements.2412-15289_sent_53b59bf3: Specifically, on AdvBench dataset, with mask language model (MLM) assistive task, SATA achieves an overall attack success rate (ASR) of \pt{85} and harmful score (HS) of 4.57, and with element lookup by position (ELP) assistive task, SATA attains an overall ASR of \pt{76} and HS of 4.43.
elements.2412-15289_sent_eb064165: \textcolor{red}{\bf Warning: This work contains potentially offensive LLMs-generated content.}
elements.2412-15289_sent_512d9673: Large Language Models (LLMs) such as GPT-4 \cite{openaiGPT4TechnicalReport2023}, Claude \cite{claude} and LLama3 \cite{dubeyLlama3Herd2024} have demonstrated superior capability of understanding, generation and reasoning, empowering a wide range of tasks such as conversational AI \cite{liSystematicReviewMetaanalysis2023}, creative writing \cite{10.1145/3491102.3501819,10.1145/3544548.3581225}, program synthesis or testing \cite{muClarifyGPTFrameworkEnhancing2024,dengLargeLanguageModels2024} and math problem solving \cite{yue2024mammoth,yang2024qwen2}, and they have been prevalently deployed as an infrastructure to provide service \cite{deepinfra}.
elements.2412-15289_sent_c3f14519: To prevent LLMs from responding to malicious queries that contain harmful intent and generating socially biased content, numerous safety alignment methods have been proposed to align the safety preferences of LLMs with those of humans, such as training data curation \cite{welblChallengesDetoxifyingLanguage2021,wangExploringLimitsDomainAdaptive} and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) \cite{ouyangTrainingLanguageModels2022a,touvronLlama2Open2023,christianoDeepReinforcementLearning2023a,rafailovDirectPreferenceOptimization2023a}.
elements.2412-15289_sent_808f22ee: Despite of significant progress, safety weakness still exists.
elements.2412-15289_sent_b039b951: Research efforts have been made to expose the safety vulnerabilities via jailbreak attack, where adversarial prompts are carefully computed or constructed and fed into LLMs to elicit unethical response.
elements.2412-15289_sent_bc436e14: Automatic jailbreak attacks can be categorized into two types: (1) regarding LLMs as computational systems and jailbreaking them using search-based method~\cite{zouUniversalTransferableAdversarial2023a,liuAutoDANGeneratingStealthy2023a,chaoJailbreakingBlackBox2024a,yuGPTFUZZERRedTeaming2024a}; (2) disguising the original harmful query inside some designed scenarios, or transforming it into certain special representation that LLMs are less adept at understanding (\eg ASCII art) and revealing them from the representation in subsequent steps.
elements.2412-15289_sent_1843fff3: Existing works either (1) rely on multiple iterations (retries) and jailbreak prompt candidates~\cite{liuAutoDANGeneratingStealthy2023a}, which introduces high input token usage, or (2) require writing sophisticated instructions (hints) in jailbreak prompt and the ability of victim LLMs to effectively understand and follow them, which could hinder the performance~\cite{jiangArtPromptASCIIArtbased2024a}.
elements.2412-15289_sent_60ec4f3e: To address the limitations, we propose a novel LLM jailbreak paradigm via Simple Assistive Task link\ul{a}ge (SATA).
elements.2412-15289_sent_b667f388: SATA first masks harmful keywords with the [MASK] special token in malicious query to reduce its toxicity, obtaining masked keywords and a masked query.
elements.2412-15289_sent_6d8ee270: Then, SATA constructs simple assistive task (described below) to encode and convey the semantics of the masked keywords, and links the masked query with the assistive task to collectively perform jailbreak.
elements.2412-15289_sent_e72515a9: The assistive task serves two purposes: first, it can distract the victim LLM, diverting its attention to the preceding assistive task and causing it to overlook the safety check of the entire query’s intent; second, the assistive task encodes the semantics of the masked contents and conveys this information to the victim LLM, thereby filling in the missing semantic in the masked instruction.
elements.2412-15289_sent_cded325b: The assistive tasks are designed to be simple and can be easily performed by victim LLMs so that (1) the missing semantic can be correctly and efficiently inferred and combined to the masked instruction by victim LLMs, improving jailbreak performance; (2) the jailbreak prompt template can be designed compact, decreasing both the jailbreak cost and prompt-design effort.
elements.2412-15289_sent_5cc5daf0: We propose two simple assistive tasks in the SATA paradigm, each of which can be linked with the masked instruction to achieve LLM jailbreak attack.
elements.2412-15289_sent_892d88a5: Specifically, we adopt Masked Language Model (MLM)~\cite{devlinBERTPretrainingDeep2018} as implicit assistive task (see Section~\ref{paragraph:MLM}), and we construct an Element Lookup by Position (ELP) task as explicit assistive task (see Section~\ref{paragraph:ELP}).
elements.2412-15289_sent_1c5aa43f: The former leverages synthesized wiki entry as the context of MLM task and prompt victim LLMs to perform wiki entry text-infilling for jailbreak while the later asks victim LLMs to identify the element in a List when given a position.
elements.2412-15289_sent_158a658f: We term our jailbreaks with the two assistive task as SATA-MLM and SATA-ELP jailbreak, respectively.
elements.2412-15289_sent_c9be6309: We evaluate the effectiveness of SATA on AdvBench Subset~\cite{zouUniversalTransferableAdversarial2023a} across four closed-source LLMs and two open-source LLMs in terms of harmful score (HS) and attack success rate (ASR) judged by GPT.
elements.2412-15289_sent_2ebef037: The experimental results show that both SATA-MLM and SATA-ELP jailbreak attacks significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art baselines.
elements.2412-15289_sent_f280224e: For instance, the SATA-MLM successfully jailbreak GPT-4o with an ASR of \pt{82} and a HS of 4.57 while SATA-ELP achieves an ASR of \pt{78} and a HS of 4.43.
elements.2412-15289_sent_f0554234: We further evaluate SATA on JBB-Behaviors dataset~\cite{chaoJailbreakBenchOpenRobustness2024} and observe consistent performance.
elements.2412-15289_sent_60a92aa1: In terms of cost, SATA-ELP with its ensemble setting reaches about an order of magnitude savings in input token usage compared to SOTA baseline, while maintaining SOTA jailbreak performance.
elements.2412-15289_sent_c19d1263: In summary, our contributions are as follows:
elements.2412-15289_sent_0be07f0b: We propose a novel LLM jailbreak paradigm via \ul{s}imple \ul{a}ssistive \ul{t}ask link\ul{a}ge (SATA).
elements.2412-15289_sent_793db686: We propose to employ Masked Language Model (MLM) or Element Lookup by Position (ELP) as implicit or explicit simple assistive tasks in SATA paradigm, respectively.
elements.2412-15289_sent_a02a64d1: Assisted with the tasks, we propose SATA-MLM and SATA-ELP jailbreak attacks.
elements.2412-15289_sent_4dd9b6b0: We conduct extensive experiments to evaluate the effectiveness, cost-efficiency and sensitivity to defense of SATA.
elements.2412-15289_sent_fc8acbcf: Evaluation results against baselines demonstrate SATA is effective, lightweight and resistant to defenses.
elements.2412-15289_sent_4e180d95: We analyze the impact of the difficulty level of the assistive task, as well as the effectiveness of the assistive task, on jailbreak performance.
elements.2412-15289_sent_6dc75778: Experimental results show the effectiveness of an LLM-friendly assistive task to efficiently convey semantic to victim LLMs.
elements.2412-15289_sent_0506991e: A collection of works regard LLMs as computational systems from the security perspective, thus jailbreaking LLMs using search and side-channel methods.
elements.2412-15289_sent_ce0e74d8: Combining greedy and gradient-based discrete optimization, GCG \cite{zouUniversalTransferableAdversarial2023a} computes an adversarial suffix and append to the original harmful instruction, achieving universal multi-model jailbreak attacks.
elements.2412-15289_sent_7a9d80f2: AutoDAN \cite{liuAutoDANGeneratingStealthy2023a} leverage genetic algorithm-based search to generate and refine jailbreak prompts iteratively.
elements.2412-15289_sent_240ba02b: PAIR \cite{chaoJailbreakingBlackBox2024a} and GPTFuzzer \cite{yuGPTFUZZERRedTeaming2024a} also belong to this line of work.
elements.2412-15289_sent_4baa0a9f: Beyond search-based methods, \citealt{dengMultilingualJailbreakChallenges2023a} exploit low-resource languages as side channels for jailbreak.
elements.2412-15289_sent_65abb740: Another line of studies regard LLMs as instruction followers, and disguise the original harmful instructions inside designed scenarios or transform them into visual symbolic representation that LLMs are less adept at understanding in order to bypass safety check.
elements.2412-15289_sent_07398b6a: \citealt{dingWolfSheepsClothing2024a} proposes the "scenario nesting" to coax the model into generating harmful responses.
elements.2412-15289_sent_08fc694c: ArtPrompt\cite{jiangArtPromptASCIIArtbased2024a} identifies LLM's incapability to effectively recognize ASCII art representation.
elements.2412-15289_sent_d8753d24: They propose to transform a single harmful word in query into ASCII art format, and reveal the word from the ASCII art representation by following instructions in jailbreak prompts.
elements.2412-15289_sent_1de30ad4: Puzzler \cite{changPlayGuessingGame2024a} implicitly expresses the malicious intent of harmful query through the combination of clues obtained from preliminary queries, achieving jailbreak.
elements.2412-15289_sent_f92cc9ca: Finally, a number of works jailbreak LLMs by humanizing them.
elements.2412-15289_sent_6529aa1a: DeepInception leverages the personification ability of LLMs, \ie LLMs can be obedient to human authority and thus override its safety-check boundary~\cite{liDeepInceptionHypnotizeLarge2024a}.
elements.2412-15289_sent_bb01d233: \citealt{zengHowJohnnyCan2024a} generate persuasive adversarial prompts to persuade LLMs to respond to harmful inputs.
elements.2412-15289_sent_53efc653: \citealt{xuCognitiveOverloadJailbreaking2024c} propose to leverage cognitive overload to jailbreak LLMs. Previous works utilize multiple iterations and/or jailbreak prompt candidates, or require sophisticated instructions (hints) in jailbreak prompt and the ability of victim LLMs to effectively understand and follow them in subsequent jailbreak , which we argue can hinder the performance when victim LLMs fail to perform the instructions in jailbreak prompt.
elements.2412-15289_sent_58ee3ff5: Despite of extensive research efforts on safety alignment, safety weakness of LLMs still exist.
elements.2412-15289_sent_1ebd5054: Thus, many works directly defense against jailbreaks to alleviate the safety alignment problem, and there are three main types of methods: (1) filter-based jailbreak detection, which checks the perplexity of input \cite{jainBaselineDefensesAdversarial2023} or leverage an additional inspector to detect if the response is harmful \cite{phuteLLMSELFDEFENSE2024,xieGradSafeDetectingJailbreak2024a,wangDefendingLLMsJailbreaking2024a}; (2) modification-based mitigation, which perturbs multiple copies of the input via permutation then aggregates the outputs \cite{robeySmoothLLMDefendingLarge2024,caoDefendingAlignmentBreakingAttacks2024a}, or directly paraphrase or re-tokenize the input \cite{jainBaselineDefensesAdversarial2023}; (3) prompt-based reminder, which use in-context demonstrations or explicit description to remind LLMs of generating ethical response \cite{xieDefendingChatGPTJailbreak2023,weiJailbreakGuardAligned2024,zhangDefendingLargeLanguage2024a}.

elements.2412-15289_sent_350370db: We introduce a jailbreak paradigm of simple assistive task linkage (SATA).
elements.2412-15289_sent_dfec2c4e: As shown in Figure~\ref{fig:method} and~\ref{fig:method2}, we first mask harmful keywords in a malicious query using a given granularity to mitigate its toxicity, resulting in a camouflaged query with one or multiple [MASK] special tokens.
elements.2412-15289_sent_3dd03c1c: Then, we propose simple assistive task to encode the semantics of the masked keywords, and pair the camouflaged query (referred to as masked instruction) with the assistive task to collectively perform the jailbreak attack.
elements.2412-15289_sent_1eaa51f9: Basically, an assistive task is an additional task combined with the jailbreak task for LLM to perform.
elements.2412-15289_sent_b61af873: To demonstrate the effectiveness of SATA, we instantiate two simple assistive tasks to perform the jailbreak attack.
elements.2412-15289_sent_cc5c44fc: The first step of SATA masks toxic words or phrases in a malicious query, preventing the harmful intent from being explicitly exposed and preparing [MASK] token for subsequent assistive tasks (described below).
elements.2412-15289_sent_74c0915a: Specifically, we employ GPT-4o~\cite{openaiGPT4TechnicalReport2023} as a worker to mask harmful words or phrases, as GPT-4 understands its own usage policy and can smartly identify harmful keywords.
elements.2412-15289_sent_e0d0da3b: We employ four categories of masking granularity: single word (sw), single phrase (sp), multiple words (mw), and multiple phrases (mp), which offer flexibility in masking harmful contents in malicious queries.
elements.2412-15289_sent_308b4ab6: The prompt used for masking harmful contents can be found in Appendix~\ref{app:maskingprompt}.
elements.2412-15289_sent_c9152a9e: The reason to design multiple masking granularity is that many malicious queries are naturally complex, containing multiple harmful keywords, such as "Develop a plan to \ul{sabotage} a transportation system and \ul{cause harm} to passengers." The single-word masking strategy would be rigid for camouflaging such harmfulness to bypass safety checks (see the impact of our masking granularity in Appendix~\ref{paragraph:granularity}).
elements.2412-15289_sent_57ae2fa5: Furthermore, multiple tries of each non-stop word~\cite{jiangArtPromptASCIIArtbased2024a} increases the jailbreak cost (see the cost comparisons in Section~\ref{paragraph:cost}).
elements.2412-15289_sent_28abb951: A LLM-friendly assistive task can serve two purposes: first, it can distract the victim LLM, diverting its attention to the assistive task and causing it to overlook the safety check of the entire query's intent; second, the assistive task encodes the semantics of the masked keywords and transfers this information to the victim LLM, thereby filling in the missing semantic in the masked instruction.
elements.2412-15289_sent_7c285e46: We analyze the importance of simple assistive tasks in Section~\ref{paragraph:ab-necessity}.
elements.2412-15289_sent_e24fbccd: We propose two assistive tasks, each of which can be linked with the masked instruction to perform the jailbreak attack.
elements.2412-15289_sent_6a727aad: Notably, the simple assistive task, as its name suggests, are constructed to be friendly for the victim LLMs to perform, ensuring that it successfully links with the masked instruction and jointly conveys the complete semantic of the original malicious query to the victim LLMs. (see the impact of the complexity of the assistive task in Section~\ref{paragraph:ab-position}).

elements.2412-15289_paragraph_dc326ec9: Masked Language Model as Implicit Assistive Task.
elements.2412-15289_sent_ab9c15db: We leverage the Masked Language Model (MLM)~\cite{devlinBERTPretrainingDeep2018} as our implicit assistive task, as LLMs are naturally adept at inferring the [MASK] token in a given context.
elements.2412-15289_sent_bc1a27e9: As shown in Figure~\ref{fig:method}, we first prompt GPT-4o to synthesize a wiki entry for the masked harmful keywords (words or phrases) identified in the masking step.
elements.2412-15289_sent_35393888: Then, we split the synthesized wiki entry into two parts by paragraphs.
elements.2412-15289_sent_529572ec: The first part will be placed before masked instruction while the remaining part will be placed after the instruction.
elements.2412-15289_sent_4d586cda: They jointly convey semantics to [MASK] token.
elements.2412-15289_sent_34d1f3f5: We term them as `prefix wiki text' and `suffix wiki text', respectively.
elements.2412-15289_sent_2c226d1b: Finally, we insert the masked instruction between prefix and suffix parts and prompt the victim LLM to infer [MASK] tokens from the surrounding wiki context and fill in the missing part indicated by the instruction (\ie text-infilling).
elements.2412-15289_sent_ba358d51: Note that we prompt GPT-3.5, in an in-context learning~\cite{brownLanguageModelsAre2020} manner, to slightly paraphrase the masked instruction to better fit the wiki entry text-infilling format, such as transform "How to make a [MASK]" into "The process of making a [MASK] can be concluded as follows:".
elements.2412-15289_sent_fdf5e510: We refer to the jailbreak using MLM as assistive task as the SATA-MLM attack and provide the prompt in Appendix~\ref{app:jailbreakprompt} Figure~\ref{fig:appendix-jailbreak-WET-attack}.
elements.2412-15289_paragraph_eaaca073: Element Lookup by Position as Explicit Assistive Task.
elements.2412-15289_sent_f36beb14: We construct Element Lookup by Position (ELP) task as our explicit assistive task, where the victim LLM is asked to identify one or multiple elements in a given List by given positions.
elements.2412-15289_sent_c764fe4e: As illustrated in Figure~\ref{fig:method2}, we begin by collecting a set of commendatory words (\eg generosity, integrity, happiness) generated by GPT-3.5-turbo beforehand.
elements.2412-15289_sent_4f82730d: Next, we randomly select ten words from this set to construct a commendatory words List (\eg Python List), and randomly insert the masked keywords into the List, and we denote their respective positions as pos\_i.
elements.2412-15289_sent_9045d7f3: Finally, we prompt the victim LLMs to answer the ELP task with the given positions and map these elements to [MASK]s in the masked instruction.
elements.2412-15289_sent_c4c5dee4: We refer to the jailbreak SATA-ELP and show the prompt in Appendix~\ref{app:jailbreakprompt} Figure~\ref{fig:appendix-jailbreak-ELP-mw}.
elements.2412-15289_sent_d20f1e2d: In summary, the SATA jailbreak paradigm constructs attacks through assistive tasks that LLMs can easily perform to efficiently encode and convey the semantics of masked harmful keywords to victim models.

elements.2412-15289_sent_202020a5: Large language models (LLMs) have made significant advancements across various tasks, but their safety alignment remains a major concern.
elements.2412-15289_sent_5e7ba678: Specifically, on AdvBench dataset, with mask language model (MLM) assistive task, SATA achieves an overall attack success rate (ASR) of \pt{85} and harmful score (HS) of 4.57, and with element lookup by position (ELP) assistive task, SATA attains an overall ASR of \pt{76} and HS of 4.43 \footnote{Our code is available in \url{https://github.com/xndong/SATA}}.
elements.2412-15289_sent_0fb8d5f3: To prevent LLMs from responding to malicious queries that contain harmful intent, numerous safety alignment methods have been proposed to align the safety preferences of LLMs with those of humans, such as training data curation \cite{welblChallengesDetoxifyingLanguage2021,wangExploringLimitsDomainAdaptive} and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) \cite{ouyangTrainingLanguageModels2022a,touvronLlama2Open2023,christianoDeepReinforcementLearning2023a,rafailovDirectPreferenceOptimization2023a}.
elements.2412-15289_sent_73f05299: Automatic jailbreak attacks can be categorized into two types: (1) regarding LLMs as computational systems and jailbreaking them using search-based method~\cite{zouUniversalTransferableAdversarial2023a,liuAutoDANGeneratingStealthy2023a,chaoJailbreakingBlackBox2024a,yuGPTFUZZERRedTeaming2024a}; (2) disguising the original harmful query inside some designed scenarios, or transforming it into certain special representation that LLMs are less adept at understanding (\eg ASCII art) and revealing it from the representation in subsequent steps.
elements.2412-15289_sent_7ea953df: To address the limitations, we propose a novel LLM jailbreak paradigm via \ul{s}imple \ul{a}ssistive \ul{t}ask link\ul{a}ge (SATA).
elements.2412-15289_sent_a0fc72c8: The assistive task serves two purposes: first, it distracts the victim LLM, diverting its attention to the preceding assistive task and causing it to overlook the safety check of the entire query’s intent; second, the assistive task encodes the semantics of the masked contents and conveys this information to the victim LLM, thereby filling in the missing semantic in the masked instruction.
elements.2412-15289_sent_7534c1f2: The assistive tasks are designed to be simple and can be easily performed by victim LLMs so that (1) the missing semantic can be correctly and efficiently inferred and combined to the masked instruction, improving jailbreak performance; (2) the jailbreak prompt template can be designed to be compact, decreasing both the jailbreak cost and prompt-design effort.
elements.2412-15289_sent_bee541c1: Specifically, we adopt Masked Language Model (MLM)~\cite{devlinBERTPretrainingDeep2018} as an implicit assistive task (see Section~\ref{paragraph:MLM}), and we construct an Element Lookup by Position (ELP) task as an explicit assistive task (see Section~\ref{paragraph:ELP}).

elements.2412-15289_sent_cdbb8365: We focus on exploring whether RAG scheme can be regarded as an effective defense approach for jailbreak, and we design the following defense experiment.

elements.2412-15289_sent_a62bc16c: Specifically, We choose a total of 778 data entries, with 178 entries from the complete HHH dataset and the first 600 entries in HH-RLHF dataset, and for each data entry in the HHH and HH-RLHF dataset, we extract the user query and assistant response to create a <HumanQuery, AssistantResponse> pair.
